title: Post-read-model locality cleanup
goal: After the read-model, attention, and projection ownership refactors, remeasure the remaining locality hotspots and perform the low-risk cleanup that still pays for itself: centralize Pi wrapper artifact paths, split daemon integration tests by domain, and extract the worker-attempt executor only if it remains the dominant manager complexity hotspot.
acceptance: All existing projection snapshots, parity fixtures, and golden output tests remain behavior-preserving unless an intentional divergence is explicitly documented in this slice output.

A short complexity evidence note records manager.rs line count and remaining hotspots after CPLX-02 through CPLX-04, including whether run_slice_worker remains worth extracting now or should be deferred.
PiWrapperArtifacts or its equivalent no longer hand-builds .herdr.* output paths outside artifact::Store; wrapper artifact path construction has one owner.
tests/daemon_integration.rs is split or reduced by domain-oriented modules/helpers for at least publication, attention, cockpit, and replan tests without weakening black-box coverage.

If worker-attempt extraction is performed, Manager keeps the lifecycle facade while attempt-local setup/handoff/launch/result-validation/status-transition behavior moves behind a typed AttemptOutcome interface; if deferred, the evidence note explains why after remeasurement.
No new shallow pass-through modules are introduced solely to reduce line counts; every new module has a small interface and demonstrable locality/depth benefit.
must_ask_if: The implementation would change worker execution semantics, retry policy, commit/merge policy, or integration gate ordering as part of locality cleanup.
The implementation would delete or skip black-box daemon integration scenarios instead of moving/splitting them.
The implementation would hide .workflow runtime layout behind an abstraction that makes legitimate external command paths harder to inspect.
The implementation would extract many shallow modules without a clear interface/depth justification.
